steps.title: Graph the answer choices
steps.content: This is faster as a Desmos check than as a full algebra problem. Graph each candidate system and compare it to the picture. The correct choice must give two downward-sloping lines that meet the $x$-axis at the same point, $(8,0)$.

steps.title: Check the shared x-intercept
steps.content: For choice D, set $y=0$. The first equation gives $4x=32$, so $x=8$. The second equation gives $-8x=-64$, so $x=8$ again. Both lines share the required $x$-intercept $(8,0)$, matching the graph.

steps.title: Check the y-intercepts
steps.content: Now set $x=0$. From $4x+10y=32$, $10y=32$, so $y=3.2$. From $-8x-10y=-64$, $-10y=-64$, so $y=6.4$. Those are the lower and upper $y$-intercepts shown in the graph.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D is the only system whose two lines share $x$-intercept $8$ and have $y$-intercepts $3.2$ and $6.4$. That matches the graph, so D is correct.
